signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CB Real Rate Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5658 anchors the week's dominant G10 theme as the USD leader-to-laggard spread hits 2.004%. Despite RBNZ hike bias, NZD remains under pressure with a -0.83% real policy rate (accommodative) vs USD +1.56% (restrictive). COT LF net short -22,454 (long% 18.4%) confirms institutional selling while retail 93% long creates extreme contrarian risk. USD outperformance is reinforced by a +29 CESI net beat score, signaling sustained US data strength.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Vol Premium + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.71 trades in the critical intervention zone identified by Scotiabank (162.00). A +264bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR vs TONA) sustains the bid despite JPY COT LF net short -112,092. Options market pricing reflects acute anxiety with IV at 7.2% vs HV30 2.3%, creating a +4.9% VRP. 25d RR at -1.20 confirms a moderate put skew as traders pay for JPY protection against potential BoJ action.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4183 exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z = -1.99σ), with the pair trending higher despite a 1.81% daily gain in crude. Retail sentiment is at a 94% short extreme, directly diverging from the COT LF net short -67,668 (23.4% long), suggesting retail is aggressively fading the institutional trend. A double top formation noted in recent headlines serves as the immediate technical resistance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6924 faces headwinds as the CESI net score of -7 reflects consistent data misses. Options markets show the highest G10 volatility risk premium at +7.0% (IV 15.2% vs HV30 8.2%), signaling extreme hedging demand. While the +73bp OIS carry favors AUD, the 25d RR at -1.11 indicates the options market remains biased toward downside protection. Watch 0.6900 for a sustained break to confirm further weakness.
